steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank is a noun phrase (*Home Ground: A Guide to the American*).

After the blank comes “by Barry Lopez and Debra Gwartney,” — a prepositional phrase describing the book's editors — followed by “the book celebrates the rich language...” — a new independent clause with its own subject and verb.

steps.title: Apply the period rule for sentence boundaries
steps.content:
A period separates two independent clauses. Choice D correctly places a period after “*Landscape*” to end the first sentence, then begins a new sentence with the introductory participial phrase “Edited by Barry Lopez and Debra Gwartney,” which properly sets up the independent clause “the book celebrates.” Choices A and C attempt to house both independent clauses in a single sentence separated only by commas, creating a comma splice; choice B uses “and edited,” which is grammatically incorrect.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
